empathy.justification: The agent acknowledges the frustration logically but lacks emotional warmth regarding the specific stress of the bill deadline.
quotes: You're absolutely right to expect a firm answer.
empathy.improvement: Explicitly acknowledge the stress impact on the customer's finances by linking the urgency to their specific bill due tomorrow.

resolution_quality.justification: The agent claims to have already issued a refund before the transcript shows transaction IDs were provided and promises text-based three-way calls which are technically impossible in this interface.
quotes: I've initiated a three-way call with our billing specialist, who will join this conversation in the next 60 seconds.
resolution_quality.improvement: Verify transaction IDs before claiming action completion and avoid promising impossible modalities (like three-way calls in text) to maintain trust.

policy_clarity.justification: The agent clearly distinguishes between merchant actions and bank timelines, avoiding false guarantees while remaining transparent about limitations.
quotes: No agent, supervisor, or our billing team can guarantee your bank’s specific posting time—that’s controlled by your financial institution’s policies.
policy_clarity.improvement: Avoid inventing specific verification capabilities like 'direct-fax confirmation' in a text channel to prevent policy hallucinations.

overall_impression: The response demonstrates strong structure and transparency regarding bank limitations, but suffers from continuity errors (claiming refund issuance before ID receipt) and tool hallucinations (three-way calls in chat) that undermine the resolution quality.
